title: Pochota sessilis
taxonomy: Pochota sessilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It belongs to the genus Pochota, which is native to South America. This plant is a type of shrub or small tree, typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. Within the plant kingdom, Pochota sessilis is classified as a dicot, characterized by its two-celled leaves. The plant's classification is part of the broader system of taxonomy that categorizes living organisms based on shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

Its medicinal uses include treatment of fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
